programme.title: The Situation Room With Wolf Blitzer
programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: Cuomo Prime Time
programme.desc: After airing several different programs in this time slot in recent years, CNN attempts to solidify its prime-time schedule by moving Chris Cuomo to the toughest hour in cable news. Cuomo, the former co-anchor of CNN's morning show `New Day', eschews panel discussions in favour of in-depth one-on-one interviews that test politicians and newsmakers on their positions. He also analyses the news of the day and presents breaking news. Described by one publication as "America's toughest news anchor", Cuomo wants his show to be known as one that holds civil debates. "The need is clear", he says. "Hopefully we help fill it".

programme.title: Don Lemon Tonight
programme.desc: Don Lemon presents the biggest stories, newsmakers, and spirited conversations of the day.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with John Vause
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with John Vause
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Rosemary Church
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Rosemary Church brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Kim Brunhuber
programme.desc: Kim Brunhuber brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: Early Start
programme.desc: An early start with news from Ashleigh Banfield and Zoraida Sambolin.

programme.title: New Day With John Berman and Brianna Keilar
programme.desc: John Berman and Brianna Keilar report the latest news and top stories.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: First Move With Julia Chatterley
programme.desc: A look at the big stories developing as the US markets open, anchored from the floor of the NYSE.

programme.title: Connect the World
programme.desc: Taking the viewers on a journey across the continents.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: Connect the World
programme.desc: Taking the viewers on a journey across the continents.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: A surge of creative energy is flowing through Rwanda where filmmakers, visual artists, entertainers and more are rewriting their own narratives and helping to shape countries future.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Peabody Award-winning journalist Christiane Amanpour, CNN's chief international correspondent, hosts this half-hour weeknight interview program, with each episode focusing on a newsworthy topic and featuring guests, from global leaders to heads of state and cultural icons, who are comfortable challenging conventional wisdom.

programme.title: Hala Gorani Tonight
programme.desc: Hala Gorani breaks down the day's news, curating the stories that matter and getting into exactly why they are important.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest and a team of correspondents analyze the facts and figures from the business world to help people earn money and spend it wisely.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.title: The Situation Room With Wolf Blitzer
programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: Cuomo Prime Time
programme.desc: After airing several different programs in this time slot in recent years, CNN attempts to solidify its prime-time schedule by moving Chris Cuomo to the toughest hour in cable news. Cuomo, the former co-anchor of CNN's morning show `New Day', eschews panel discussions in favour of in-depth one-on-one interviews that test politicians and newsmakers on their positions. He also analyses the news of the day and presents breaking news. Described by one publication as "America's toughest news anchor", Cuomo wants his show to be known as one that holds civil debates. "The need is clear", he says. "Hopefully we help fill it".

programme.title: Don Lemon Tonight
programme.desc: Don Lemon presents the biggest stories, newsmakers, and spirited conversations of the day.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Michael Holmes
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Michael Holmes presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: Marketplace Africa
programme.desc: Offering unique insights into African market's latest information while observing the events and developments that impact the business world.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: Reports on African political and social issues from host Isha Sesay and correspondents across the continent.

programme.title: Connecting Africa
programme.desc: From the CEO of a massive energy company in South Africa and fruit exporters in Egypt to the man who bought a bank in Nigeria, looking at all of the aspects of doing business in Africa.

programme.title: Africa Avant Garde
programme.desc: A look at some of the most influential living modern and contemporary artists as well as a visit to a museum which celebrates the best of contemporary African art.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Michael Holmes
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Michael Holmes presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: Marketplace Africa
programme.desc: A look at the economy and industry surrounding textiles in Kenya and highlighting Laureen Kouassi-Olsson, a seasoned executive in the African financial services and private equity industries.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Michael Holmes
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Michael Holmes presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: An exploration of the changemakers in Africa who are coming up with innovative ways to have a positive impact on their communities.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Robyn Curnow
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Robyn Curnow presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Kim Brunhuber
programme.desc: Kim Brunhuber brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: Connecting Africa
programme.desc: From the CEO of a massive energy company in South Africa and fruit exporters in Egypt to the man who bought a bank in Nigeria, looking at all of the aspects of doing business in Africa.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: Reports on African political and social issues from host Isha Sesay and correspondents across the continent.

programme.title: New Day Saturday
programme.desc: The latest news reports and top stories.

programme.title: New Day Saturday
programme.desc: The latest news reports and top stories.

programme.title: Smerconish
programme.desc: After serving as an MSNBC contributor and guest-host on the network for four years, TV and radio personality Michael Smerconish believed he was ready to head a show of his own. CNN agreed, and thus was born `Smerconish', an hourlong Saturday morning program that takes after its host's independent views. Using a topical, conversational format, he aims for the TV show to be an extension of his weekday radio broadcast, with free-flowing topics and the ability to discuss issues that strike a chord with the audience. Along the same lines, he thinks it's important to present the content in an engaging manner, free of my-way-or-the-highway ideology. Smerconish, who has a law degree from the University of Pennsylvania, is also a New York Times' best-selling author.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom Saturday
programme.desc: Updates of the latest news from around the world.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom Saturday
programme.desc: Updates of the latest news from around the world.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci eats and drinks his way around Milan. Here, Stanley enjoys an aperitivo, feels inspired to make pizzoccheri, a noodle made from buckwheat, and enjoys classic Milanese veal chops and risotto.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: From superbikes to racecars and beyond, highlighting some of South Africa's fastest men and women on wheels and providing an inside look at what life is like on and off the race track.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: Following a Kenyan media producer who is on a mission to make the superhero genre more inclusive. Highlighting the visionary behind South Africa's first superhero comic book series.

programme.title: Africa Avant Garde
programme.desc: A look at some of the most influential living modern and contemporary artists as well as a visit to a museum which celebrates the best of contemporary African art.

programme.title: Marketplace Africa
programme.desc: A look at the contemporary African art market. Given the wide range of applications, looking at how satellites are part of the solution when it comes to solving the connectivity gap in Africa.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Michael Holmes
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Michael Holmes presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: Connecting Africa
programme.desc: From the CEO of a massive energy company in South Africa and fruit exporters in Egypt to the man who bought a bank in Nigeria, looking at all of the aspects of doing business in Africa.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: Following a Kenyan media producer who is on a mission to make the superhero genre more inclusive. Highlighting the visionary behind South Africa's first superhero comic book series.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Michael Holmes
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Michael Holmes presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Kim Brunhuber
programme.desc: Kim Brunhuber brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: Africa Avant Garde
programme.desc: A look at some of the most influential living modern and contemporary artists as well as a visit to a museum which celebrates the best of contemporary African art.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: Following a Kenyan media producer who is on a mission to make the superhero genre more inclusive. Highlighting the visionary behind South Africa's first superhero comic book series.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci explores his former home: Tuscany. Stanley does a wine bar crawl, celebrates the importance of the lowly loaf and samples dishes that use stale bread as the key ingredient.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.title: State of the Union With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: Newsmakers and Washington power players discuss current issues and events, with additional commentary by a panel of reporters and analysts. Hosted by Jake Tapper, the Sunday morning program also goes beyond the Beltway for a perspective on how everyday Americans are faring in today's world.

programme.title: Fareed Zakaria GPS
programme.desc: Host, columnist, and best-selling author Fareed Zakaria offers insightful analysis and interviews world leaders, artists, scientists, business leaders, global affairs analysts, and other cultural observers for the `Global Public Square'. Zakaria is also a syndicated columnist for The Washington Post, a contributing editor for The Atlantic magazine, and hosts in-depth, prime-time specials for CNN Worldwide. `Fareed Zakaria GPS' was launched in 2008 and each week's program airs worldwide on CNN, from New York or other locations around the world. The program is characterized by smart debate and discussion on world events and public affairs, designed to help viewers understand the world around them and the newest thinking on the globe's biggest challenges.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci explores his former home: Tuscany. Stanley does a wine bar crawl, celebrates the importance of the lowly loaf and samples dishes that use stale bread as the key ingredient.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: From superbikes to racecars and beyond, highlighting some of South Africa's fastest men and women on wheels and providing an inside look at what life is like on and off the race track.

programme.title: Connecting Africa
programme.desc: From the CEO of a massive energy company in South Africa and fruit exporters in Egypt to the man who bought a bank in Nigeria, looking at all of the aspects of doing business in Africa.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: Following a Kenyan media producer who is on a mission to make the superhero genre more inclusive. Highlighting the visionary behind South Africa's first superhero comic book series.

programme.desc: Stanley Tucci explores his former home: Tuscany. Stanley does a wine bar crawl, celebrates the importance of the lowly loaf and samples dishes that use stale bread as the key ingredient.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Kim Brunhuber
programme.desc: Kim Brunhuber brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom
programme.desc: Daily headlines and breaking stories live from CNN headquarters in Atlanta. The rotating cast of anchors and contributors include Carol Costello, Kyra Phillips, Suzanne Malveaux, Brooke Baldwin, Don Lemon, Randi Kaye and Fredricka Whitfield.

programme.title: Early Start
programme.desc: An early start with news from Ashleigh Banfield and Zoraida Sambolin.

programme.desc: John Berman and Brianna Keilar report the latest news and top stories.

programme.desc: Taking the viewers on a journey across the continents.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: Following a Kenyan media producer who is on a mission to make the superhero genre more inclusive. Highlighting the visionary behind South Africa's first superhero comic book series.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Peabody Award-winning journalist Christiane Amanpour, CNN's chief international correspondent, hosts this half-hour weeknight interview program, with each episode focusing on a newsworthy topic and featuring guests, from global leaders to heads of state and cultural icons, who are comfortable challenging conventional wisdom.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci explores his former home: Tuscany. Stanley does a wine bar crawl, celebrates the importance of the lowly loaf and samples dishes that use stale bread as the key ingredient.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest and a team of correspondents analyze the facts and figures from the business world to help people earn money and spend it wisely.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.title: The Situation Room With Wolf Blitzer
programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

programme.title: CNN Town Hall
programme.desc: President Joe Biden participates in a town hall in Cincinnati, Ohio. Anchor Don Lemon moderates the live event focusing on a wide range of issues facing the nation ranging from Covid-19 to the economy.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci explores his former home: Tuscany. Stanley does a wine bar crawl, celebrates the importance of the lowly loaf and samples dishes that use stale bread as the key ingredient.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with John Vause
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with John Vause
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Rosemary Church
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Rosemary Church brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom
programme.desc: Daily headlines and breaking stories live from CNN headquarters in Atlanta. The rotating cast of anchors and contributors include Carol Costello, Kyra Phillips, Suzanne Malveaux, Brooke Baldwin, Don Lemon, Randi Kaye and Fredricka Whitfield.

programme.title: Early Start
programme.desc: An early start with news from Ashleigh Banfield and Zoraida Sambolin.

programme.title: New Day With John Berman and Brianna Keilar
programme.desc: John Berman and Brianna Keilar report the latest news and top stories.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: First Move With Julia Chatterley
programme.desc: A look at the big stories developing as the US markets open, anchored from the floor of the NYSE.

programme.title: Connect the World
programme.desc: Taking the viewers on a journey across the continents.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: Connect the World
programme.desc: Taking the viewers on a journey across the continents.

programme.title: One World with Zain Asher
programme.desc: Offering a fresh and inclusive take on the top stories from Africa, Europe and beyond, giving every continent an equal voice.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: From superbikes to racecars and beyond, highlighting some of South Africa's fastest men and women on wheels and providing an inside look at what life is like on and off the race track.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Peabody Award-winning journalist Christiane Amanpour, CNN's chief international correspondent, hosts this half-hour weeknight interview program, with each episode focusing on a newsworthy topic and featuring guests, from global leaders to heads of state and cultural icons, who are comfortable challenging conventional wisdom.

programme.title: Hala Gorani Tonight
programme.desc: Hala Gorani breaks down the day's news, curating the stories that matter and getting into exactly why they are important.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest and a team of correspondents analyze the facts and figures from the business world to help people earn money and spend it wisely.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.title: The Situation Room With Wolf Blitzer
programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: Don Lemon Tonight
programme.desc: Don Lemon presents the biggest stories, newsmakers, and spirited conversations of the day.

programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: From superbikes to racecars and beyond, highlighting some of South Africa's fastest men and women on wheels and providing an inside look at what life is like on and off the race track.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Peabody Award-winning journalist Christiane Amanpour, CNN's chief international correspondent, hosts this half-hour weeknight interview program, with each episode focusing on a newsworthy topic and featuring guests, from global leaders to heads of state and cultural icons, who are comfortable challenging conventional wisdom.

programme.title: Hala Gorani Tonight
programme.desc: Hala Gorani breaks down the day's news, curating the stories that matter and getting into exactly why they are important.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest and a team of correspondents analyze the facts and figures from the business world to help people earn money and spend it wisely.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.title: The Situation Room With Wolf Blitzer
programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: CNN Special Report
programme.desc: Bill Weir takes viewers to high tech farms, food labs, and chef's tables across America to find out how farmers, chefs, and fishermen have adapted in the modern world and interrogates the future of food production.

programme.title: Don Lemon Tonight
programme.desc: Don Lemon presents the biggest stories, newsmakers, and spirited conversations of the day.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Michael Holmes
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Michael Holmes presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: Marketplace Africa
programme.desc: Offering unique insights into African market's latest information while observing the events and developments that impact the business world.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: Reports on African political and social issues from host Isha Sesay and correspondents across the continent.

programme.title: Living Golf
programme.desc: Previewing the Olympic golf competition with a look at the history of golf in the Olympic Games. Plus, a look at Japan's unique golfing culture.

programme.title: Quest's World of Wonder
programme.desc: Perched on the rocky cliffs above a shimmering sea are medieval ramparts, nearly perfectly intact, protecting a gem inside. Exploring the real heart and soul of Dubrovnik in Croatia.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Michael Holmes
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Michael Holmes presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: Marketplace Africa
programme.desc: A look at the contemporary African art market. Given the wide range of applications, looking at how satellites are part of the solution when it comes to solving the connectivity gap in Africa.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Michael Holmes
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Michael Holmes presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: An exploration of the changemakers in Africa who are coming up with innovative ways to have a positive impact on their communities.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Robyn Curnow
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Robyn Curnow presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Kim Brunhuber
programme.desc: Kim Brunhuber brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: Marketplace Asia
programme.desc: While spectators will be barred from attending the Tokyo Olympic Games, billions across the world will be using technology to tune in, making the Olympic Games a space for innovation.

programme.title: Marketplace Europe
programme.desc: Anna Stewart takes a looks at the companies developing boundary breaking technologies at the Goodwood Festival's Future Lab and speaks to the head of Space Systems for Airbus, Jean-Marc Nasr.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: Reports on African political and social issues from host Isha Sesay and correspondents across the continent.

programme.title: New Day Saturday
programme.desc: The latest news reports and top stories.

programme.title: Smerconish
programme.desc: After serving as an MSNBC contributor and guest-host on the network for four years, TV and radio personality Michael Smerconish believed he was ready to head a show of his own. CNN agreed, and thus was born `Smerconish', an hourlong Saturday morning program that takes after its host's independent views. Using a topical, conversational format, he aims for the TV show to be an extension of his weekday radio broadcast, with free-flowing topics and the ability to discuss issues that strike a chord with the audience. Along the same lines, he thinks it's important to present the content in an engaging manner, free of my-way-or-the-highway ideology. Smerconish, who has a law degree from the University of Pennsylvania, is also a New York Times' best-selling author.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom Saturday
programme.desc: Updates of the latest news from around the world.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: Living Golf
programme.desc: Previewing the Olympic golf competition with a look at the history of golf in the Olympic Games. Plus, a look at Japan's unique golfing culture.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Robyn Curnow
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Robyn Curnow presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Kim Brunhuber
programme.desc: Kim Brunhuber brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: Living Golf
programme.desc: Previewing the Olympic golf competition with a look at the history of golf in the Olympic Games. Plus, a look at Japan's unique golfing culture.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: An exploration of the changemakers in Africa who are coming up with innovative ways to have a positive impact on their communities.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci visits Sicily. Here, Stanley tries a salty and crunchy sensation, tastes the grapes grown in the region's unique soils, and reflects on the nature of Sicilian hospitality.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.title: State of the Union With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: Newsmakers and Washington power players discuss current issues and events, with additional commentary by a panel of reporters and analysts. Hosted by Jake Tapper, the Sunday morning program also goes beyond the Beltway for a perspective on how everyday Americans are faring in today's world.

programme.title: Fareed Zakaria GPS
programme.desc: Host, columnist, and best-selling author Fareed Zakaria offers insightful analysis and interviews world leaders, artists, scientists, business leaders, global affairs analysts, and other cultural observers for the `Global Public Square'. Zakaria is also a syndicated columnist for The Washington Post, a contributing editor for The Atlantic magazine, and hosts in-depth, prime-time specials for CNN Worldwide. `Fareed Zakaria GPS' was launched in 2008 and each week's program airs worldwide on CNN, from New York or other locations around the world. The program is characterized by smart debate and discussion on world events and public affairs, designed to help viewers understand the world around them and the newest thinking on the globe's biggest challenges.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci visits Sicily. Here, Stanley tries a salty and crunchy sensation, tastes the grapes grown in the region's unique soils, and reflects on the nature of Sicilian hospitality.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: Reports on African political and social issues from host Isha Sesay and correspondents across the continent.

programme.title: Marketplace Europe
programme.desc: Anna Stewart takes a looks at the companies developing boundary breaking technologies at the Goodwood Festival's Future Lab and speaks to the head of Space Systems for Airbus, Jean-Marc Nasr.

programme.title: Marketplace Asia
programme.desc: While spectators will be barred from attending the Tokyo Olympic Games, billions across the world will be using technology to tune in, making the Olympic Games a space for innovation.

programme.desc: An exploration of the changemakers in Africa who are coming up with innovative ways to have a positive impact on their communities.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci visits Sicily. Here, Stanley tries a salty and crunchy sensation, tastes the grapes grown in the region's unique soils, and reflects on the nature of Sicilian hospitality.

programme.desc: Updates of the latest news from around the world.

programme.title: CNN Special Report
programme.desc: Don Lemon reviews the most popular TV theme songs in history with Gary Portnoy of `Cheers', Christopher Knight from 'The Brady Bunch', Fran Drescher from `The Nanny', and the composer of a multitude of black TV theme songs, Kurt Farquhar.

programme.desc: Stanley Tucci visits Sicily. Here, Stanley tries a salty and crunchy sensation, tastes the grapes grown in the region's unique soils, and reflects on the nature of Sicilian hospitality.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.title: Marketplace Africa
programme.desc: Offering unique insights into African market's latest information while observing the events and developments that impact the business world.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Peabody Award-winning journalist Christiane Amanpour, CNN's chief international correspondent, hosts this half-hour weeknight interview program, with each episode focusing on a newsworthy topic and featuring guests, from global leaders to heads of state and cultural icons, who are comfortable challenging conventional wisdom.

programme.title: Hala Gorani Tonight
programme.desc: Hala Gorani breaks down the day's news, curating the stories that matter and getting into exactly why they are important.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest and a team of correspondents analyze the facts and figures from the business world to help people earn money and spend it wisely.

programme.title: Living Golf
programme.desc: Previewing the Olympic golf competition with a look at the history of golf in the Olympic Games. Plus, a look at Japan's unique golfing culture.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.title: The Situation Room With Wolf Blitzer
programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: Cuomo Prime Time
programme.desc: After airing several different programs in this time slot in recent years, CNN attempts to solidify its prime-time schedule by moving Chris Cuomo to the toughest hour in cable news. Cuomo, the former co-anchor of CNN's morning show `New Day', eschews panel discussions in favour of in-depth one-on-one interviews that test politicians and newsmakers on their positions. He also analyses the news of the day and presents breaking news. Described by one publication as "America's toughest news anchor", Cuomo wants his show to be known as one that holds civil debates. "The need is clear", he says. "Hopefully we help fill it".

programme.title: Don Lemon Tonight
programme.desc: Don Lemon presents the biggest stories, newsmakers, and spirited conversations of the day.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with John Vause
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with John Vause
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Robyn Curnow presents the latest news and global events.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.title: African Voices: Changemakers
programme.desc: An exploration of the changemakers in Africa who are coming up with innovative ways to have a positive impact on their communities.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Peabody Award-winning journalist Christiane Amanpour, CNN's chief international correspondent, hosts this half-hour weeknight interview program, with each episode focusing on a newsworthy topic and featuring guests, from global leaders to heads of state and cultural icons, who are comfortable challenging conventional wisdom.

programme.title: Hala Gorani Tonight
programme.desc: Hala Gorani breaks down the day's news, curating the stories that matter and getting into exactly why they are important.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest and a team of correspondents analyze the facts and figures from the business world to help people earn money and spend it wisely.

programme.title: Living Golf
programme.desc: Previewing the Olympic golf competition with a look at the history of golf in the Olympic Games. Plus, a look at Japan's unique golfing culture.

programme.title: The Lead With Jake Tapper
programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.title: The Situation Room With Wolf Blitzer
programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

programme.title: Anderson Cooper 360
programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.title: Cuomo Prime Time
programme.desc: After airing several different programs in this time slot in recent years, CNN attempts to solidify its prime-time schedule by moving Chris Cuomo to the toughest hour in cable news. Cuomo, the former co-anchor of CNN's morning show `New Day', eschews panel discussions in favour of in-depth one-on-one interviews that test politicians and newsmakers on their positions. He also analyses the news of the day and presents breaking news. Described by one publication as "America's toughest news anchor", Cuomo wants his show to be known as one that holds civil debates. "The need is clear", he says. "Hopefully we help fill it".

programme.title: Don Lemon Tonight
programme.desc: Don Lemon presents the biggest stories, newsmakers, and spirited conversations of the day.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with John Vause
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with John Vause
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: CNN Newsroom with Rosemary Church
programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, Rosemary Church brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world.

programme.title: Early Start
programme.desc: An early start with news from Ashleigh Banfield and Zoraida Sambolin.

programme.title: New Day With John Berman and Brianna Keilar
programme.desc: John Berman and Brianna Keilar report the latest news and top stories.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci visits Sicily. Here, Stanley tries a salty and crunchy sensation, tastes the grapes grown in the region's unique soils, and reflects on the nature of Sicilian hospitality.

programme.title: New Day With John Berman and Brianna Keilar
programme.desc: John Berman and Brianna Keilar report the latest news and top stories.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: First Move With Julia Chatterley
programme.desc: A look at the big stories developing as the US markets open, anchored from the floor of the NYSE.

programme.title: Marketplace Europe
programme.desc: Anna Stewart takes a looks at the companies developing boundary breaking technologies at the Goodwood Festival's Future Lab and speaks to the head of Space Systems for Airbus, Jean-Marc Nasr.

programme.title: Connect the World
programme.desc: Taking the viewers on a journey across the continents.

programme.title: World Sport
programme.desc: Highlights and action from the world's sporting competitions.

programme.title: Connect the World
programme.desc: Taking the viewers on a journey across the continents.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.desc: An exploration of the changemakers in Africa who are coming up with innovative ways to have a positive impact on their communities.

programme.desc: Peabody Award-winning journalist Christiane Amanpour, CNN's chief international correspondent, hosts this half-hour weeknight interview program, with each episode focusing on a newsworthy topic and featuring guests, from global leaders to heads of state and cultural icons, who are comfortable challenging conventional wisdom.

programme.title: Stanley Tucci: Searching for Italy
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci visits Sicily. Here, Stanley tries a salty and crunchy sensation, tastes the grapes grown in the region's unique soils, and reflects on the nature of Sicilian hospitality.

programme.desc: Richard Quest and a team of correspondents analyze the facts and figures from the business world to help people earn money and spend it wisely.

programme.desc: Anna Stewart takes a looks at the companies developing boundary breaking technologies at the Goodwood Festival's Future Lab and speaks to the head of Space Systems for Airbus, Jean-Marc Nasr.

programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.title: The Situation Room With Wolf Blitzer
programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
programme.desc: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.desc: Stanley Tucci visits Sicily. Here, Stanley tries a salty and crunchy sensation, tastes the grapes grown in the region's unique soils, and reflects on the nature of Sicilian hospitality.

programme.title: Inside Politics
programme.desc: With veteran Washington correspondent John King in the anchor chair, `Inside Politics' focuses on the top political stories of the day with a rotating round table of top sourced reporters from CNN and from other media outlets. The half-hour program gives viewers an inside look at the stories driving Washington, directly from reporters on the ground. Prior to its revival in 2014, `Inside Politics' was first headed by Judy Woodruff, and it aired for more than 20 years on CNN before its cancellation in 2005.

programme.title: Inside Africa
programme.desc: Reports on African political and social issues from host Isha Sesay and correspondents across the continent.

programme.title: Amanpour
programme.desc: Peabody Award-winning journalist Christiane Amanpour, CNN's chief international correspondent, hosts this half-hour weeknight interview program, with each episode focusing on a newsworthy topic and featuring guests, from global leaders to heads of state and cultural icons, who are comfortable challenging conventional wisdom.

programme.title: Hala Gorani Tonight
programme.desc: Hala Gorani breaks down the day's news, curating the stories that matter and getting into exactly why they are important.

programme.title: Quest Means Business
programme.desc: Richard Quest and a team of correspondents analyze the facts and figures from the business world to help people earn money and spend it wisely.

programme.desc: CNN's chief Washington correspondent, Jake Tapper, hosts this hour-long weekday afternoon program, which covers all the day's top stories from around the country and the world, headlines that span politics, money, sports and popular culture. `The Lead' works to ensure that viewers are caught up on the stories that matter, often highlighting news that may have been overlooked. Prior to joining CNN, Tapper served as senior White House correspondent at ABC News.

programme.desc: Anchor Wolf Blitzer invites CNN correspondents, analysts and guests for timely coverage of the day's news events. Patterned after the concept of the White House Situation Room, the Monday-Saturday show is a discussion forum often with opposing sides debating current national affairs.

programme.desc: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

programme.desc: After airing several different programs in this time slot in recent years, CNN attempts to solidify its prime-time schedule by moving Chris Cuomo to the toughest hour in cable news. Cuomo, the former co-anchor of CNN's morning show `New Day', eschews panel discussions in favour of in-depth one-on-one interviews that test politicians and newsmakers on their positions. He also analyses the news of the day and presents breaking news. Described by one publication as "America's toughest news anchor", Cuomo wants his show to be known as one that holds civil debates. "The need is clear", he says. "Hopefully we help fill it".

programme.desc: Don Lemon presents the biggest stories, newsmakers, and spirited conversations of the day.

programme.desc: Live from Atlanta, John Vause brings context and clarity to the day's top stories from around the world, followed by the latest sporting news.
